name: Rohan
description: The Riddermark — the great grasslands between the White Mountains and the Fangorn eaves, reaching north to the Limlight and west to the Isen. Big skies, bigger weather. Continental through and through: hot dry summers, cold hard winters, thunderstorms that can be seen coming for an hour across the plain.

spring.label: Fair and keen, the grass bright with new green, larks rising from every hollow.
A steady wind off the White Mountains — the grass silvered in long rolling waves.
Fast-moving showers, visible for miles before they arrive and after they leave.
Thunder walking across the plain, lightning striking far off on the horizon.
Morning mist in the river-valleys, the open plain above clear under the rising sun.
Grey overcast and a cold hard wind — the Mark at its most unwelcoming in spring.
A full day of steady rain — the plain dark and running, the horse-lords philosophical.
A last wet snowfall, unseasonable — melting within the day, leaving the grass flat and bright.

summer.label: Hot and dry, the sun fierce, the grass gone gold under a clean blue sky.
Warm with a steady breeze, good travelling weather across open country.
Hazy heat, the mountains blurred in the south, dust-devils on the horizon.
A great plains thunderstorm — black cloud from horizon to horizon, rain in sheets.
Storm visible for an hour before it comes — lightning forking down over distant hills.
A hot dry wind off the south, the grass crackling, water sources already low.
A welcome cool rain after heat — every stream running high in an hour.
Cool and brilliantly bright — a rare day, the kind the horse-lords remember.

autumn.label: Crisp and clear, the plain gone gold and russet, the White Mountains sharp in the south.
A cold wind off the north, the grass flattened in long running waves.
Heavy mist on the plain until mid-morning, the herds lost in it until it lifts.
Steady cold rain from the west, the plain dark and running with it.
An autumn storm off the mountains — driving rain, thunder close, the horses uneasy.
The first hard frost, grass and puddle alike glazed at dawn.
Flat grey overcast, the light failing early, the year turning.
The first early snow on the highest ground — a warning from the north.

winter.label: Hard cold and a low grey sky — the Mark at its longest and least forgiving.
Snow falling steadily, the plain turning white from horizon to horizon.
A bitter wind off the north, drift-snow running like smoke along the ground.
A true plains blizzard — visibility gone, the wind vicious, shelter everything.
Clear and brutally cold, a pale sun giving no warmth, the grass ice-white.
Freezing fog rising from the plain, the cold settling in for days.
Sleet and half-snow, the trampled ground around the halls a churn of mud and ice.
A still deep cold, no wind, every fire burning straight up into a hard blue dusk.
